title: 10 best herbal essential oils for depression
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for depression are natural remedies extracted from plants that help alleviate symptoms of depression by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving mood. The best essential oils for depression include Lavandula angustifolia, Melissa officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Valeriana officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, Rosmarinus officinalis, Santalum album, Boswellia serrata, Ginkgo biloba, and Avena sativa. If left untreated, depression can severely impact daily life, causing individuals to withdraw from social interactions, experience sleep disturbances, and lose interest in activities they once enjoyed, ultimately affecting their relationships, work, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with depression because it has a calming and soothing effect on the mind and body. The sweet, floral scent of lavender oil can slow down breathing and heart rate, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety levels. This, in turn, can help alleviate symptoms of depression such as irritability, mood swings, and insomnia. Additionally, the aromatic properties of lavender oil can also uplift and calm the emotions, promoting a sense of serenity and tranquility that is essential for managing depressive episodes.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Lavender oil's anxiolytic and sedative properties help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can alleviate symptoms of depression by calming the mind and body.

Mood-Boosting Aromachemicals: The aroma of lavender oil contains mood-boosting compounds such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain, helping to improve mood and reduce symptoms of depression.

Antidepressant Activity: Lavender oil has been found to have antidepressant activity due to its ability to inhibit the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which can help regulate mood and alleviate symptoms of depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression.
Flowers: Lavender flowers contain linalool, a natural mood stabilizer that has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning when oil content is highest.
Dry the harvested flowers for 10-14 days in a well-ventilated area protected from direct sunlight and moisture.
Combine the dried lavender flowers with 500 ml of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a glass container at room temperature for 14 days to allow for slow diffusion and extraction of essential oils.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids and store the extracted essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with depression because of its uplifting and calming properties. The oil, derived from the leaves of the lemon balm plant, has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, which are often underlying causes of depression. The sedative effects of lemon balm essential oil can help regulate sleep patterns, while its mood-boosting properties can promote a sense of calm and well-being, reducing feelings of sadness and hopelessness. This natural remedy can be used in aromatherapy, massage therapy, or added to bath water for a relaxing and rejuvenating experience.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant activity: Lemon balm essential oil has been shown to possess antidepressant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of depression by reducing anxiety and stress levels.

Anxiolytic properties: The anxiolytic compounds present in lemon balm essential oil have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and panic attacks that are often associated with depression.

GABA-uptake inhibition: Lemon balm essential oil has been found to inhibit the uptake of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep, relaxation, and mood balance. By blocking the reabsorption of GABA, lemon balm essential oil can help increase its levels in the brain, promoting feelings of calmness and reducing symptoms of depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce symptoms of depression by calming the nervous system.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are rich in antioxidants and have a sedative effect, making them effective in promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety associated with depression.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn or early morning when essential oil content is highest.
Rinse the harvested plant material with distilled water to remove dirt and debris then air dry for 24 hours.
Blend one part dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers with five parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a dark glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days allowing the essential oils to infuse into the carrier oil slowly.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean amber glass bottle and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with depression because it contains limonene, a compound that has been shown to have a significant impact on mood regulation. The scent of bitter orange essential oil can stimulate the production of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which are often imbalanced in individuals suffering from depression. This can help alleviate symptoms of low mood, anxiety, and fatigue, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Citrus Limonoids: Bitter orange essential oil contains citrus limonoids, which have been shown to have antidepressant effects by increasing serotonin levels in the brain, helping to improve mood and reduce symptoms of depression.

Volatile Organic Compounds (VOCs): The VOCs present in bitter orange essential oil can help to reduce stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, which can contribute to a sense of despair often associated with depression.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as naringenin and hesperidin, found in bitter orange essential oil have been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain that may contribute to depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bitter orange roots contain bioactive compounds like limonene and flavonoids, which have been shown to have antidepressant properties.
Barks: The barks of the bitter orange tree are rich in alkaloids, including citral and geraniol, which have a calming effect on the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh bitter orange peel from organic citrus x aurantium trees.
Dry the harvested peels in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Combine 30g of dried bitter orange peels with 300ml of hexane in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 48 hours, shaking the jar every 12 hours.
Filter the resulting liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then evaporate the solvent using a rotary evaporator or slow distillation to yield 10ml of citrus x aurantium essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort essential oils helps with depression because it contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have a positive effect on neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine. This can help alleviate symptoms of depression by promoting feelings of happiness and well-being. Additionally, the oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the brain associated with depression. The uplifting and calming scent of St John's Wort essential oils can also promote relaxation and improve mood.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant effects: St. John's Wort essential oil has been shown to have antidepressant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of depression by increasing serotonin and dopamine levels in the brain.

Anxiolytic effects: The oil has anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, which can help calm nervousness and anxiety that often accompanies depression, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing stress.

Anti-inflammatory effects: St. John's Wort essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the brain and body, often associated with depression, and promote overall mental well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a higher concentration of hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to have antidepressant properties.
Flowers: The flowers are rich in essential oil and contain flavonoids, which are known for their mood-boosting and stress-relieving effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers at dawn, ensuring optimal potency and aroma retention.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm oven set to 60°c for 6 hours to preserve their active compounds.
Load a glass jar with 100 grams of dried st john's wort flowers, then add 200 milliliters of high-proof solvent-free ethanol.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2 weeks, allowing for maximum extraction of essential oils and active constituents.
Filter the mixture through a coffee filter to separate the oil from solids, discarding the residue and transferring the extracted oil to a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame essential oils helps with depression because it has a profound impact on the mind and body. The oil's sesamol compound has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. Additionally, sesame oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of depression such as fatigue and mood swings. By incorporating sesame essential oils into daily routines, individuals may experience improved mental clarity, reduced symptoms of depression, and a more positive outlook on life.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sesame essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, inflammation, and mood disorders like depression.

Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects: The anxiolytic compounds in sesame essential oil, such as sesamin and sesamolin, have been shown to reduce anxiety-like behaviors and improve mood by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain.

Adaptogenic properties: Sesame essential oil's adaptogenic properties help to balance the body's response to stress, reducing feelings of anxiety, fatigue, and depression by modulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sesame roots contain sesamolin and sesamin, which have been shown to have a positive effect on mood and anxiety.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of the sesame plant contains sesamin and sesamolin, which are believed to have anti-depressant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh sesamum indicum leaves at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested leaves in shade for 3-4 days to prevent oxidation and bacterial growth.
Steam distill the dried sesamum indicum leaves in a clevenger apparatus with 200ml of water for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the aqueous phase using a separating funnel and store it in dark glass bottle.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 24 hours before use to permit settling of impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with depression because its sweet, floral aroma has a profound impact on the mind and emotions. The oil's calming properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility that can be particularly beneficial for individuals struggling with depression. Additionally, the uplifting scent can boost mood and energy levels, helping to alleviate feelings of sadness and hopelessness associated with depression.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant: Damask rose essential oil contains a compound called geraniol, which has been shown to have antidepressant effects by increasing the production of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, helping to alleviate symptoms of depression.

Anxiolytic (Anxiety-reducing): The oil's calming and sedative properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for depressive episodes, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels in the body.

Mood-boosting: The fragrance molecules present in damask rose essential oil, such as linalool and beta-damascenone, have been shown to stimulate the brain's limbic system, which is responsible for emotions and mood regulation, helping to uplift and improve one's mood.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the damask rose plant contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have antidepressant properties and are often used in traditional medicine to treat anxiety and depression.
Buds: Damask rose buds are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate symptoms of depression.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rose petals from rosa damascena plants at dawn or just before sunset.
Dry the harvested rose petals in a warm and dry place for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 50 grams of dried rose petals in a clevenger apparatus for 4-6 hours using water at 100°c.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the aqueous phase by decantation or centrifugation.
Store the rosa damascena essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with depression because of its unique composition, which contains anethole, a potent natural mood booster. When inhaled or applied topically, this oil stimulates the brain's neurotransmitters, releasing feel-good hormones like serotonin and dopamine, which are often depleted in individuals struggling with depression. Additionally, fennel's calming and soothing properties help reduce anxiety and stress, common comorbidities of depression, promoting a sense of relaxation and overall well-being. This natural remedy can be a valuable adjunct to traditional treatments, offering hope for those seeking relief from depressive symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) property: Fennel oil's anxiolytic property helps to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often contribute to depression.

Mood-boosting property: The aroma compounds present in fennel oil, such as anethole and limonene, have been shown to stimulate the release of neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine, which regulate mood and can help alleviate symptoms of depression.

Inflammation-reducing property: Fennel oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the body, which has been linked to depression. By reducing inflammation, fennel oil may help improve overall mental health and reduce symptoms of depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain sesquiterpenes, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help alleviate symptoms of depression.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in anethole, a compound known for its mood-boosting properties and ability to reduce anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal oil yield.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by rinsing with filtered water and air-drying for 24 hours to prevent contamination.
Steam-distill the dried foeniculum vulgare plant material using a clevenger apparatus with 5 liters of distilled water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Filter the essential oil from the distillation mixture through a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and measuring the yield as 2% volume.
Bottle the foeniculum vulgare essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids and store it at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime essential oils helps with depression because of its uplifting and calming properties, which can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and sadness. The citrus scent of lime oil has been shown to stimulate the brain's production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that helps regulate mood, reducing symptoms of depression. Additionally, the aroma of lime oil can also help to improve focus and mental clarity, allowing individuals to better cope with stress and emotions.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant properties: Herbal lime essential oil contains compounds like citral and limonene, which have been shown to have a positive impact on mood and reduce symptoms of depression by stimulating the production of neurotransmitters like serotonin.

Anxiolytic properties: The aroma of herbal lime essential oil has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels, which are often associated with depression.

Mood-enhancing properties: The uplifting scent of herbal lime essential oil can help increase feelings of joy, happiness, and relaxation, making it a natural mood booster that can help alleviate symptoms of depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them useful for treating anxiety and depression.
Barks: The barks of the lime plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of depression.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia x europaea flowers at dawn when they are in full bloom, gathering approximately one ounce per batch.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator set to low heat for four to six hours or by air drying them for two weeks.
Combine one cup of dried tilia x europaea flowers with 12 cups of carrier oil such as olive or jojoba in a large glass jar, stirring well.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the herbal extracts to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, squeezing the solids gently to extract every last drop of essential oil before transferring it to glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with depression because it has a profound effect on mood and cognitive function. The menthol content in peppermint oil can increase alertness, boost energy levels, and enhance mental clarity, helping to combat feelings of lethargy and hopelessness that often accompany depression. Additionally, the invigorating aroma of peppermint oil can uplift and calm the mind, reducing stress and anxiety that contribute to depressive episodes.
remedy_properties: Mood Uplifting Properties: Peppermint oil's mood-boosting compounds like menthol and menthone have a stimulating effect on the mind, helping to improve mental clarity and reduce symptoms of depression.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is often associated with depression, leading to improved mood and reduced feelings of sadness.

Anxiolytic Properties: The anxiolytic compounds in peppermint oil, such as limonene, have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate anxiety and stress that can exacerbate depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate symptoms of depression.
Bark: The bark of peppermint contains menthol, a natural analgesic and anxiolytic that helps to reduce stress and anxiety associated with depression.
Seeds: Peppermint seeds contain flavonoids, which have been found to have antidepressant properties by increasing serotonin levels in the brain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves at dawn on a sunny day to ensure optimal oil content.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill 200g of clean peppermint leaves in 500ml of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Collect and separate the resulting essential oil from the steam condensate using a glass separating funnel.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities or residue within 1 hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony essential oils helps with depression because they possess a unique ability to uplift and balance the mind-body connection. The oil's sweet, floral scent is said to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels. Its antidepressant properties also help to boost mood-boosting neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, promoting feelings of happiness and relaxation. Additionally, peony essential oils have been shown to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted in individuals suffering from depression.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (Anti-Anxiety): The essential oil has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels which can contribute to depression.

Mood-Elevating: Peony essential oil contains compounds like benzaldehyde and linalool, which have been found to stimulate the release of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, helping to improve mood and reduce symptoms of depression.
Adaptogenic (Stress-Reducing): The oil's adaptogenic properties help the body respond more effectively to stress, reducing feelings of overwhelm and fatigue that can exacerbate depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess antidepressant and anxiolytic properties, making them suitable for the production of medicinal essential oils.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been linked to improved mood and reduced symptoms of depression.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried paeonia lactiflora roots from a reputable supplier or your own garden after 6 months of drying.
Crush the dried roots into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their natural oils.
Combine the crushed root pieces with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar, seal it tightly, and store it in a cool dark place for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, squeezing the roots to extract as much liquid as possible before discarding them.
Transfer the extracted liquid to a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for at least 30 days before using, allowing the natural compounds to settle and mature.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat depression with herbal essential oils is by combining bergamot oil's uplifting properties with lavender oil's calming effects. Mix equal parts of both oils and apply topically to pulse points or inhale through a diffuser. Ylang-ylang oil also helps alleviate symptoms by promoting feelings of relaxation and self-worth. Blend these oils with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba for safe use.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to depression that are treated with herbal essential oils are anxiety, stress, insomnia, and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). Certain essential oils such as lavender, chamomile, and bergamot have a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing symptoms of anxiety and stress. Oils like vetiver and ylang-ylang help alleviate feelings of sadness and low mood associated with depression.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with depression because of its calming and mood-boosting properties. The oil's unique composition of linalool and linalyl acetate has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety levels by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality. As a result, individuals suffering from depression may experience a decrease in symptoms and an overall sense of well-being. Additionally, the oil's uplifting scent can help alleviate feelings of sadness and hopelessness, promoting a more positive outlook and emotional balance.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of depression by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene ester has a sedative effect, which can help individuals with depression by promoting better sleep quality and reducing stress levels.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has been shown to have anxiolytic properties, which can help individuals with depression by reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting a sense of calmness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lavandula angustifolia leaves are used to make essential oils because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of depression.
Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia flowers are used to make essential oils because they are rich in calming and mood-boosting properties, such as linalool and linalyl acetate.
Stems: Lavandula angustifolia stems are used to make essential oils because they contain a higher percentage of linalool, which is known for its antidepressant and anxiolytic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning to capture their peak fragrance and potency.
Steam distill 100g of freshly harvested flowers with 1l of water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Separate the distilled essential oil from the hydrosol through a condenser coil to obtain a clear liquid.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities or debris from the distillation process.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to preserve its quality and shelf life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with depression because it contains lemon balm, a natural mood elevator that promotes relaxation and reduces stress levels. The oil's calming properties help to soothe an overactive mind and promote a sense of calmness, making it easier to manage symptoms of depression. Additionally, the oil's ability to improve sleep quality and reduce anxiety also contributes to its antidepressant effects, providing a natural and holistic approach to managing depression. Its gentle yet effective properties make it a popular choice for those seeking natural relief.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Acts as a mood stabilizer, reducing anxiety and stress levels by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
Linalool: Exhibits anxiolytic and sedative properties, helping to reduce symptoms of depression by promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses antioxidant properties, which help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation, two key factors contributing to depression.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Melissa officinalis for essential oil production due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has antidepressant properties.

Flowers: Melissa officinalis flowers are also used to produce essential oils that help alleviate depression symptoms, as they contain compounds like citronellal and geraniol, which have mood-boosting effects.

Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis contain a high amount of citral, a compound that has been shown to have antidepressant properties and is often used in aromatherapy to reduce stress and anxiety related to depression.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers when the plant is in full bloom for optimal oil production.
Dry the plant material in a warm place or using a dehydrator at 95°f for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a distillation apparatus at 100°f for 2 hours to release essential oil.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a separatory funnel and a clean glass vial.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils helps with depression because it contains a unique combination of compounds that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The oil's sedative properties help calm the mind and body, making it easier to manage stress and negative thoughts. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata has been shown to increase the production of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and GABA, which play a crucial role in regulating mood. This natural remedy can provide relief from depressive symptoms and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isovitexin: Isovitexin has flavonoid properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, contributing to improved mood and reduced symptoms of depression.
Verbascoside: Verbascoside is a phenylethanoid glycoside with anxiolytic and sedative effects, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which are crucial in managing depression.

Lignans: Lignans have a neuroprotective effect and help to modulate the activity of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which play a significant role in regulating mood and emotional responses.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in sedative and antispasmodic properties that help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system and may help alleviate symptoms of depression.
Barks: Have been traditionally used to treat anxiety and insomnia, and the essential oil may help reduce stress and promote a sense of calm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a clean glass container to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Filter the collected essential oil using a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with depression because of its sedative and anxiolytic properties, which promote relaxation and calmness. The oil contains valerenic acid, a compound that interacts with the brain's GABA receptors to reduce stress and anxiety levels. This leads to a decrease in cortisol production and an increase in serotonin, a neurotransmitter that regulates mood. As a result, individuals experiencing depression may find relief from their symptoms, such as improved sleep, reduced irritability, and enhanced overall sense of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps with depression by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Valeranone: It contributes to the antidepressant effects by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters like serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine, thereby improving mood.

Valeranone acetate: It exerts antidepressant properties by modulating the activity of various neurotransmitters, including serotonin and GABA, which helps regulate mood and reduce symptoms of depression.
Isovaleric acid: It also plays a role in alleviating depressive symptoms by acting on the brain's GABA system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Valerenal: It is another constituent found in Valeriana officinalis essential oil that contributes to its antidepressant effects by modulating the activity of various neurotransmitters and influencing the brain's GABA system.

Valeranone epoxide: It also has a role in the antidepressant effects of Valeriana officinalis essential oil by interacting with the brain's GABA system and influencing the activity of various neurotransmitters.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are the most used part to make essential oils for depression due to their high content of valerenic acid, which is responsible for its sedative and anxiolytic properties.
Flowers: Valerian flowers are used to make essential oils for depression as they contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which contribute to their calming and relaxing effects.
Stems: Valerian stems are also used to make essential oils for depression, as they contain a mixture of sesquiterpenes and esters that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and stems in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Steam distill 100g of dried valeriana officinalis roots and stems for 3 to 4 hours to release essential oils.
Collect and filter the distilled essential oil to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight fitting lid to preserve its quality.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 2 weeks before using it for medicinal purposes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils helps with depression because it contains a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have a positive impact on mental health. The oil's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, are believed to inhibit the reuptake of neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine, thereby promoting a sense of well-being and reducing symptoms of depression. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support mood and emotional balance, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to managing depression.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a potent monoamine reuptake inhibitor, which helps increase the levels of neurotransmitters like serotonin and norepinephrine in the brain, thereby alleviating symptoms of depression.
N-feruloylserotonin: Exhibits antidepressant properties by promoting the release of neurotransmitters and inhibiting their reuptake, leading to improved mood and reduced depressive symptoms.

Quercetin: Functions as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to the development and progression of depression.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain the highest concentration of flavonoids and naphthoquinones, which are responsible for their antidepressant and mood-boosting properties.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, contributing to their antidepressant effects.
Buds: They contain a higher concentration of hyperforin, a key compound responsible for the antidepressant and mood-stabilizing properties of Hypericum perforatum.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 to 40 flowers in the morning to capture optimal oil content from mature buds.
Clean 250 grams of hypericum flowers to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Steam distill the cleaned flowers for 2 hours at 2.5 bars pressure to extract oil.
Separate the oil from the distilled water using a separator or decantation method.
Filter the extracted oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove impurities and improve clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with depression because it has a profound impact on emotional well-being. The fragrance of rosemary oil is known to boost mood and reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression. The oil's ability to improve cognitive function and memory can also help alleviate feelings of hopelessness. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, rosemary oil can create a sense of calm and clarity, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing depression. This can lead to improved overall mental health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: Helps with depression by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help regulate mood and reduce anxiety.
Carvacrol: Contributes to reducing depression by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with depression.
Rosmarinic acid: Aids in alleviating depression by inhibiting the activity of monoamine oxidase enzymes, which can help regulate the levels of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for its calming and mood-boosting properties, helping to reduce symptoms of depression.
Flowers: Utilized for its uplifting and stress-relieving effects, promoting relaxation and improving mood.
Stems: Employed for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of depression related to stress and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems in the early morning to maximize oil content and flavor.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at a temperature of 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water through a process of cooling and decanting slowly.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and improve clarity rapidly.
Store the final product in dark glass bottles and label them properly to maintain quality and freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album essential oils helps with depression because it possesses a unique combination of compounds that promote emotional balance and calmness. The oil's woody and earthy aroma is known to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress, allowing individuals to feel more grounded and centered. Its mood-boosting properties also help alleviate symptoms of depression, such as sadness and hopelessness, by promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being. Regular use of this oil can lead to improved mental clarity and a more positive outlook on life.
remedy_constituents: Santalol: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help in reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, contributing to the management of depression.
α-Santalol: Possesses sedative properties, which can help in reducing stress and promoting a sense of calmness, ultimately aiding in the treatment of depression.
β-Santalol: Exhibits antidepressant activity by modulating the levels of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, helping to alleviate symptoms of depression.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make essential oils for depression because they contain a high concentration of santalol, a compound known to have calming and mood-boosting properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make essential oils for depression because they contain a mix of sesquiterpenes and sesquiterpene alcohols that help reduce anxiety and stress.
Barks: Barks are used to make essential oils for depression because they contain a high percentage of santalol and other sesquiterpenes that promote relaxation and reduce symptoms of depression.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves and twigs during the early morning to minimize contamination from morning dew and heat.
Steam distill the harvested plant material for 2 hours to extract the essential oils using a steam distillation apparatus.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a sterile filter paper to remove any impurities and contaminants immediately.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata essential oils helps with depression because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce stress and anxiety by blocking the production of stress hormones like cortisol. This results in a calming effect, allowing individuals to relax and focus on the present moment. The oil's adaptogenic properties also help regulate mood and promote a sense of balance and well-being, making it easier to manage symptoms of depression and improve overall mental health. It has been traditionally used to alleviate emotional distress.
remedy_constituents: Alpha-boswellic acid: It helps with depression by modulating the levels of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which are involved in mood regulation.
Beta-boswellic acid: It helps with depression by reducing inflammation in the brain, which is often associated with depression, and also by modulating the levels of neurotransmitters.
Terpineol: It helps with depression by its anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia often associated with depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antidepressant properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (or underground stems) contain high concentrations of boswellic acids, making them a key component in essential oils for depression.
Barks: The barks are another source of boswellic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of depression.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of boswellia serrata roots and dry them in a cool environment for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried roots in a still with 2% water at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation process and separate it from the water using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a 100-micron filter to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep them in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with depression because of its ability to improve blood flow to the brain, increasing oxygenation and reducing inflammation. This can help alleviate symptoms of depression by promoting a healthy brain environment. The oil's antioxidant properties also help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to depression. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to enhance neurotransmitter function can help regulate mood and reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. This can lead to improved mental clarity and emotional balance.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with depression by acting as antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress in the brain, and promoting neuroprotective effects.
Bilobalide: Help with depression by modulating neurotransmitter activity, particularly increasing the levels of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine, which are associated with improved mood.
Ginkgolides: Help with depression by inhibiting the platelet-activating factor (PAF), a pro-inflammatory mediator that contributes to depression, anxiety, and other mental health issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree are primarily used to make essential oils for depression due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antidepressant properties.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of ginkgolides, which have been found to have neuroprotective effects and may help alleviate symptoms of depression.
Roots: The roots are used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to their potential use in managing symptoms of depression.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees at the end of their growing season.
Steam distill the leaves in a still at a temperature of 100-150 degrees celsius for 4-6 hours.
Collect 10-15 ml of the distilled oil and filter it using a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency.
Allow the oil to mature for 2-3 weeks before using it to allow its properties to stabilize.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils helps with depression because it contains calming and soothing properties that promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The oil's sedative properties help to calm the mind and body, alleviating symptoms of anxiety and depression. Additionally, Avena sativa oil has been shown to regulate mood by influencing the body's hormonal balance. By promoting a sense of calm and balance, Avena sativa essential oil can help individuals manage depression and improve their overall well-being. This natural remedy offers a gentle and non-invasive approach to emotional healing.
remedy_constituents: Oleuropein: A polyphenolic compound that has been shown to have anxiolytic and antidepressant effects by modulating neurotransmitter activity and reducing oxidative stress.
Furanocoumarin: A class of compounds that have been found to possess sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of depression by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Lignans: A type of polyphenol that has been shown to have a positive impact on the gut-brain axis, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and promoting the production of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for essential oils to help alleviate depression due to their calming and soothing properties.
Seeds: Utilized in essential oils to reduce anxiety and stress associated with depression.
Leaves: Employed in essential oils to promote relaxation and reduce symptoms of depression.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh avena sativa flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has fallen for highest essential oil content.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days at 35-40°c temperature.
Steam distill the dried avena sativa plant material in a 10:1 water to plant material ratio for 2-3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water through decantation and filtration using activated charcoal.
Store the obtained avena sativa essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at 15-20°c temperature.
